25.reason: [fscanf with %s reads space-delimited tokens, but the problem defines a word as only alphabetic characters. This approach fails to correctly extract words from tokens that contain non-alphabetic characters (e.g., "don't" becomes "don" and "t" incorrectly or not at all). Also, if file is NULL, this causes a crash.]

69.reason: [The word is incremented here, but later the same word is unconditionally added again to WN[numofwords], leading to duplicate entries and incorrect counts. The logic for checking existing words is applied after adding the word, which is backwards.]

73.reason: [This copies the word into WN before checking if it already exists. So even if the word exists, a duplicate entry is created, and then the existence check sets flag=1 but doesn't prevent the duplicate from being counted via numofwords++.]

83.reason: [Because the word was already copied into WN[numofwords] before this check, when flag==1 (word exists), the duplicate remains in the array but numofwords isn't incremented. However, the earlier loop already incremented the count of the existing word, so now there are two entries: one correct (with incremented count) and one new (with count=1) that shouldn't exist. This corrupts the frequency count and output.]
